rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an apparent argumentative inconsistency between a reported drop in self-preference and claims of a positive monotone relationship, so cleanup centrally includes logical coherence.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation implies aligning the interpretation with the reported numerical results and controls without changing the experimental design.
rationale.R1.6: The phrase "Clean this up" most directly requests sentence- and paragraph-level improvement in clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any cleanup should preserve uncertainty around causal interpretation, mediation, and cross-model generalization.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact makes strong causal and mitigation claims, so avoiding overclaiming is actively required.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within cleaning the research prose rather than auditing novelty, redesigning experiments, or expanding scholarship.
rationale.RX.4: Because the passage discusses mitigation strategies for evaluation pipelines, cleanup should avoid introducing misleading downstream recommendations.
rationale.RX.5: The revised responsibility must preserve traceability to the reported figures, controls, and stated protocol.
